architecture.encoder_stages: NormConv1d stem, four EncoderBlock stages, Snake1d, final NormConv1d
architecture.bottleneck: VAEBottleneck with in_proj mean/scale and out_proj back to decoder channels
architecture.decoder: quantizer.out_proj followed by DACVAE Decoder and optional watermark branch
architecture.watermark_policy: Irodori disables the watermark branch for this codec by setting decoder.alpha=0 and replacing decoder.watermark with wm_model.encoder_block.forward_no_conv

runtime_semantics.encode_input: mono waveform B,1,samples; resample to codec sample_rate; optional -16.0 dB loudness normalization; reflect-pad to hop_length multiple
runtime_semantics.encode_output: mean latent from quantizer.in_proj, transposed to B,T,32 for Irodori runtime
runtime_semantics.decode_input: B,T,32 runtime latent transposed to B,32,T for DACVAE
runtime_semantics.deterministic_encode: uses quantizer.in_proj mean and ignores sampled scale
runtime_semantics.deterministic_decode: bypasses the upstream watermark branch for Irodori by keeping decoder.alpha=0 and replacing decoder.watermark with decoder.wm_model.encoder_block.forward_no_conv when present

required_logical_tensors.shape_notes: 1-D convolutional weights; stem maps 1 channel to encoder_dim, downsample stages follow encoder_rates [2,4,8,8], final channel count feeds quantizer.in_proj
logical Conv1d from internal encoder channels to 64 channels; split into mean[32] and scale[32]
logical Conv1d from 32 latent channels back to internal decoder channels
transposed-convolution/residual stack following decoder_rates [8,8,4,2], with watermark submodules present but bypassed for Irodori

shared_checkpoint_families.base_v2: uses the same 32-dim codec for speaker/reference encode and final decode
shared_checkpoint_families.voicedesign_v2: uses the same 32-dim codec for final decode; speaker/reference branch is disabled
shared_checkpoint_families.v3: uses the same 32-dim codec and adds duration-predictor RF-DiT weights

unknowns_and_blockers: The public codec is distributed as a PyTorch weights.pth file, not safetensors; a real converter must inspect torch state_dict keys after loading the exact dacvae version.
The lightweight MLX fixture codec in this repository is not an acoustic Semantic-DACVAE conversion and must not be treated as parity evidence.
Exact MLX parity requires fixed waveform/latent fixtures covering encode mean latents, decode waveform shape/range, watermark bypass, and tail padding behavior.
Redistribution of converted codec weights remains subject to the repository license/distribution policy and must stay separate from test fixtures.
